# Setnayan ‚Äî Changelog

Append-only log of every meaningful code change. Newest at top. Each entry includes a `SPEC IMPACT` callout (even if "None") so spec-folder edits via Cowork are never missed.

---

## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ feat(std-reveal): rigid family ‚Üí real three.js 3D scene + one-light VSM shadows (faithful-rebuild PR3a/4)

PR3 of the Save-the-Date faithful rebuild, staged on a 4-agent architecture pass (map seam ¬∑ three.js conventions ¬∑ scene design ¬∑ texture pipeline). **PR3a = the real-time WebGL conversion + the locked ¬ß1a lighting/shadow rig**; PR3b (photoreal PBR textures + church-door wood) follows. Replaces the flat CSS-3D rigid flaps (four-flap ¬∑ two-flap-v/h ¬∑ church-doors) with an **actual three.js scene** so the three ¬ß1a shadow types (form shading ¬∑ flap-cast-on-paper ¬∑ seam occlusion) fall out of real geometry + light, not hand-faking.

- **New `reveal/rigid-webgl.tsx`** ‚Äî a back invitation PAPER plane (receiveShadow) + per-template flap **meshes** (two-sided: paper front, liner back via stacked double-sided meshes) hinged in groups, driven by the same `progress` 0..1 (read live via a ref so palette/scrub never tears down the GL context). **ONE soft centre `SpotLight`** + a `HemisphereLight` fill, **`VSMShadowMap`** ‚Äî carrying the owner's locked picker values verbatim as `LIGHT_LOCK` (DIAMETER 5 ‚Üí `shadow.radius=5`/`blurSamples=12` ¬∑ DIFFUSION 100 ‚Üí `penumbra=1.0`+`hemi‚âà1.1` ¬∑ BRIGHTNESS 50 ‚Üí `intensity‚âà1.4`). Orthographic head-on camera (flat envelope, symmetric flaps + shadows). `progress` drives each flap's rotation via smoothstep + staggered relay windows to fold ~158‚Äì160¬∞ (doors 138¬∞) fully off-frame ‚Üí the ¬ß1a clean shadowless end state, with an exposure bloom to "best light" at full open. **Parallax moves ONLY the light** (gyro/pointer; paper + flaps locked ‚Üí shadows slide), off under `prefers-reduced-motion`. Colours read live from the same `--color-cream`/`--color-terracotta`/`--color-mulberry` Mood-Board vars `buildSitePaletteVars` overrides (‚Ç±0 recolour, no new threading). The PBR texture-map slots are wired for PR3b.
- **New `reveal/rigid-flaps.tsx`** ‚Äî the WebGL-or-CSS switch. Lazy-loads `rigid-webgl` via `next/dynamic(ssr:false)` so **three.js stays code-split** (verified by a production build: three lives only in lazy chunks; the 200 KB shared-bundle gate is untouched). Falls back to the existing CSS flaps when WebGL is unavailable **or** on `?reveal3d=off` (an A/B escape hatch). `four-flap`/`rigid-reveal` now pass their existing progress-driven CSS JSX as the `cssFallback` ‚Äî so the fallback is byte-identical to today and **RigidStage's gesture/seal/scroll/onOpened engine is untouched** (zero regression).
- **Verification:** I can't render WebGL in this sandbox, so this went through a 2-agent code-correctness review (three.js API + scene math; integration + bundle + fallback). It caught a **critical** bug ‚Äî several flaps' fold-direction signs were inverted (folding behind the opaque paper ‚Üí blank reveal); fixed by deriving the correct `maxDeg` sign per flap from the rotation matrices, then **re-verified by an independent agent: all 10 flaps now lift toward the camera and fold off-frame.** Also fixed: resize-staleness (scene rescales in x to stay full-bleed) + shadow-cam near. `tsc` 0 ¬∑ `next lint` clean ¬∑ production build green (three code-split confirmed).

Flag-gated reveal unchanged (`NEXT_PUBLIC_STD_REVEAL` default OFF ‚Üí zero live impact). **‚öÝ The WebGL LOOK is eyeball-only on a real GPU ‚Äî please review on the Vercel preview** (`?reveal=four-flap|two-flap-vertical|two-flap-horizontal|church-doors`; `?reveal3d=off` to A/B against the CSS version) ‚Äî it is likely to want a tuning pass (light/shadow/fold timing are all `LIGHT_LOCK`/window constants). The CSS reveal remains the safety net.

SPEC IMPACT: realises `0024_ADDENDUM ¬ß1a` (real 3D scene ¬∑ one centre light ¬∑ VSM shadow maps ¬∑ two-sided flaps ¬∑ parallax-on-shadows) for the rigid family. No SKU/schema/pricing/public-surface change. PR3b = the ¬ß1a TRUE-TEXTURE photoreal maps + church-door wood. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ feat(std-reveal): the Candle Stamp Maker ‚Äî couples mint their wax seal (faithful-rebuild PR2/4)

Owner: "the monogram created will be the stamp used to create the wax seal." PR2 of the Save-the-Date faithful rebuild builds the **candle stamp maker** (`0024_ADDENDUM ¬ß3` + `Wax_Seal_Creation_Rules_2026-06-15.md`): the couple's monogram is the brass **die**, which they press into molten wax to **mint a one-of-a-kind seal**. Designed via a 10-agent map+design+judge+synthesize workflow against the live code.

**Architecture ‚Äî deterministic recipe, ‚Ç±0, renders identically in maker + live reveal:**
- New column `events.wax_seal_config jsonb` (migration `20270109000483`, additive ¬∑ nullable ¬∑ length-capped) stores a tiny **recipe** `{v, seed, wax{color,finish}, pour{amount,irregularity,bubbles}, press{crispness,depth,offset,skew}, mark, isDefault}` ‚Äî **not an image**. The monogram die + wax colour are read **live** (`monogram_uploaded_svg ?? monogram_custom_svg`; Mood-Board deep accent), so the seal recolours for free and never bakes per-couple pixels. RLS: the existing `couple_can_update_event` policy is the enforcement (no new policy); the action gates on explicit couple membership then writes RLS-scoped.
- New `lib/wax-seal/{types,paint}.ts` ‚Äî `types` = the `WaxSealConfig` + `sanitizeWaxSealConfig` (clamps/whitelists/hex-validates) + `mulberry32` seeded PRNG + `fallbackSeedFromPublicId` + `resolveWaxColor` + `defaultConfigFromSeed`; `paint` = the deterministic **Canvas-2D `paintWaxSeal`** (seeded self-levelled puddle, monogram pressed in as a raised emboss, per-pour rim bulge + overheat micro-bubbles, matte/glossy sheen ‚Äî **no drop shadow**, ¬ß1a) + `buildMarkCanvas` (vector marks ‚Üí alpha silhouette; **raster `<image>` uploads ‚Üí luminance-threshold height field**, fixing PR1's raster‚Üíblank limitation). 8 unit tests (`wax-seal.test.ts`) lock determinism + the sanitizer.
- `reveal/wax-seal.tsx` **refactored** CSS-mask ‚Üí a `<canvas>` calling `paintWaxSeal`, with the prior CSS render kept as the pre-hydration / no-JS static fallback (never blank). The recipe threads through the existing reveal relay (`RevealOverlay` ‚Üí rigid family ‚Üí `RigidStage` ‚Üí `WaxSeal`) + the dashboard preview as two new props (`config`/`fallbackSeed`), defaulting to a `public_id`-seeded bespoke seal when un-minted ‚Äî so **every couple gets a one-of-a-kind seal from day one**; the maker is authoring, never a gate.
- The maker (`add-ons/save-the-date/stamp/`): a server page + the `wax-stamp-maker.tsx` ritual ‚Äî **pour** (hold to grow the puddle / tap for default; over-hold seeds overheat bubbles) ‚Üí **the 3-zone consistency window** (the one skill beat ‚Äî a needle dwells in a green "ready" band) ‚Üí **press** the die straight down (no twist gesture exists, so the no-twist craft rule holds by construction) ‚Üí **set** ‚Üí **confirm** (wax-colour swatches from the Mood Board + matte/glossy + "Pour again" / "Use this seal"). Never-stuck: tap-pours, the needle dwells, idling auto-presses a perfect seal, an always-present one-tap "mint a clean seal", and `prefers-reduced-motion` skips straight to a crisp default. `saveWaxSeal`/`clearWaxSeal` server actions (cloned from the cipher-monogram pattern). A "Make your wax seal" CTA on the Save-the-Date page.

Migration applied to prod via `supabase db query` (statement-by-statement + manual ledger row ‚Äî `supabase db push` is blocked by a pre-existing `20270110000000` parallel-session ledger drift; flagged for owner, not touched here). Flag-gated reveal unchanged (`NEXT_PUBLIC_STD_REVEAL` default OFF). Verified: `tsc` 0 ¬∑ `next lint` clean ¬∑ `test:unit` wax-seal 8/8. Adversarial review pass to follow (as in PR1).

**‚öÝ Surfaced for owner (recommended defaults shipped):** (1) `reveal/wax-seal.tsx` moved CSS‚ÜíCanvas ‚Äî the merged PR1 seal's look changes slightly (the upgrade that makes minting reproducible + fixes raster emboss); (2) un-minted couples now get a `public_id`-seeded bespoke seal rather than a plain default disc; (3) the maker lives at `‚Ä¶/save-the-date/stamp` (a child route).

SPEC IMPACT: new `events.wax_seal_config` column + the candle-stamp maker surface (free, no SKU). Aligns to `0024_ADDENDUM ¬ß3` (wax seal from the monogram, ‚Ç±0) + the wax-seal craft rules. No pricing/SKU/public-marketing change. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`.
## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ refactor(nav): customer-menu SSOT tree + ONE generalized section sub-nav (redesign PR1/6)

Owner: *"sub nav are child menus of the 6 menus ‚Ä¶ we are redesigning how the customer menu is"* ‚Üí all 6 menus get child sub-navs, unified into one hierarchy (plan `adaptive-forging-lobster.md`). **PR1 is the foundation, behavior-preserving:** it introduces the canonical tree and collapses the two bespoke section docks into one config-driven component. Stacks on the sub-nav-first PR (#1593).

- **NEW `lib/customer-menu.ts` ‚Äî the single canonical hierarchy.** `buildCustomerMenuTree(eventId, ctx)` returns the 6 parents (Home¬∑Guests¬∑Explore¬∑Studio¬∑Design¬∑Budget), each able to own `children`. In PR1 only **Guests** (children sourced from `lib/guest-journey.ts`, `kind:'route'`) and **Explore** (from `lib/budget-build.ts` `BUDGET_BUILD_TABS`/`TAB_META`, `kind:'tab'`) carry children ‚Äî the other four are parents-without-children until their PRs. A child is either `route` (separate page ‚Üí router.push, active by longest-prefix `match`) or `tab` (in-page panel ‚Üí `replaceState(?tab=)` + the `BB_TAB_EVENT` bus). Each parent carries BOTH a broad `activeMatch` (the bottom-nav tab, consumed in PR4) and a narrow `sectionMatch`/`sectionMatchExact` (where the dock shows): Guests = prefix over `/guests`+`/seating` (mirrors `isGuestJourneyPath`); Explore = exact `/vendors` (mirrors the old `isTakeoverRoot`). Helpers `matchesMenuSection` + `activeRouteChildKey`. Neutral module (no `'use client'`) so server + client both import it.
- **NEW `customer-section-subnav.tsx` ‚Äî ONE dock replaces two.** Reads the tree, resolves the active parent via `usePathname()`, renders that parent's children through the shared `<SubNav>`, and dispatches per child `kind` (routed ‚Üí router.push; tab ‚Üí replaceState + `goToBuildTab`). Preserves verbatim: the Guests Day-of mute/time-gate (deferred to an effect, SSR-safe), the Explore `?tab=` seeding + `BB_TAB_EVENT` listener (so Compare‚ÜíBuild / Build‚ÜíLock keep it lit), `dockedCount`‚Üíicons-only coordination, `.subnav-lift`-on-entry, the shared `html.subnav-docked` clearance, and `null` when the active parent has no children. **Deletes** `guests-section-subnav.tsx` + `vendors-section-subnav.tsx`; the layout now mounts the single `<CustomerSectionSubnav>` next to `<CustomerBottomNav>`.
- No change to the bottom nav, the sidebar, or any page; `guest-journey.ts`/`budget-build.ts` stay the per-domain single-sources (the tree composes them). Guests (routed) + Explore (tabs) behave identically to before.

Verified in the worktree: `tsc --noEmit` 0 ¬∑ `next lint` clean (changed files) ¬∑ bottom-nav template guard ‚úì. Authed surface needs prod env ‚Üí owner confirms on the Vercel preview.

SPEC IMPACT: minor ‚Äî establishes the canonical customer-menu hierarchy (6 parents ‚Üí children) as one SSOT and unifies the section-sub-nav implementation; no SKU/schema/pricing/public change. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md` (0021/0022). Next: PR2 Design+Budget children ¬∑ PR3 Home+Studio ¬∑ PR4 sidebar unify ¬∑ PR5 phase fold-in ¬∑ PR6 registry parent-link.

## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ feat(explore): sub-nav renders + responds first ‚Äî lift the takeover section nav into the event layout

Owner: *"when we open explore, the first that should load is the sub nav before the full screen. the sub nav should always respond first."* The in-app **Explore** tab (`/dashboard/[eventId]/vendors` ‚Üí the Services "Build" takeover) blocks behind the full-screen loading cover (`vendors/loading.tsx` ‚Äî spinner + "Downloading your information‚Ä¶") while the ~48KB server `page.tsx` fetches all five slots; the 5-stage `<SubNav>` (Summary ¬∑ Shortlist ¬∑ Build ¬∑ Compare ¬∑ Lock) lived **inside** `ServicesTakeover`, which the server builds last, so it only painted **after** every query resolved. Now the sub-nav paints + is tappable the instant Explore opens, with the panel streaming in behind it.

- **Moved the dock up to the event layout** (the proven `<GuestsSectionSubnav>` pattern, #1503). New client component `app/dashboard/[eventId]/_components/vendors-section-subnav.tsx`, mounted once in `dashboard/[eventId]/layout.tsx` next to `<CustomerBottomNav>` + `<GuestsSectionSubnav>`. A layout renders **above** the page segment's `<Suspense>`/`loading.tsx` boundary and **persists across the loading‚Üípage swap**, so the shelf mounts once (the `.subnav-lift` reveal fires on section ENTRY only) and is interactive while the page data is still in flight. Self-gates with `usePathname()` to the exact takeover root (`/dashboard/[eventId]/vendors`) ‚Üí `null` on `/categories`, `/packages`, vendor-detail, and every other tab (no double-stack).
- **One route, client-state tabs** (unlike the Guests journey's separate routes), so the dock drives switching over the existing **`BB_TAB_EVENT`** bus: `onSelect` ‚Üí mirror into `?tab=` via `replaceState` + dispatch the event, which `ServicesTakeover`'s listener consumes to switch the panel (no server round-trip). The dock also LISTENS to the bus so cross-tab jumps (Compare "Modify" ‚Üí Build, Build ‚Üí Lock via `goToBuildTab`) keep it lit. Active tab seeds from `?tab=` (read off `window`, not `useSearchParams` ‚Äî `replaceState` doesn't update that hook), default `'summary'` to match `page.tsx`'s no-`?tab=` fallback.
- **Bus + meta lifted to `lib/budget-build.ts`.** `BB_TAB_EVENT`, `goToBuildTab`, and `TAB_META` (label/icon/blurb) now live next to `BUDGET_BUILD_TABS` so the dock (layout subtree) and the takeover (page subtree) share one source without a cross-`_components` import. `services-takeover.tsx` **re-exports** `BB_TAB_EVENT`/`goToBuildTab` so the imperative consumers (`build-compare.tsx`, `build-picks-list.tsx`) are untouched, **removes** its own inline `<SubNav>` (mandatory ‚Äî keeping it would double the pill + `dockedCount`), and now seeds its initial tab from the live `window` `?tab=` (deferred to a mount effect ‚Üí no hydration flash) so a tab tapped *during* the load is honored when the panel finally mounts.
- **Bottom-clearance:** the dock flags `<html class="subnav-docked">` while on-screen; `globals.css`'s `[data-shell-main]` padding rule now matches `subnav-docked` alongside the Guests `guests-subnav-docked` (Guests untouched). Desktop unaffected (`<SubNav>` is `lg:hidden`; the takeover keeps its desktop top strip). Guests (`/guests*`,`/seating*`) and Explore (`/vendors`) gate on mutually-exclusive paths ‚Üí `dockedCount` is only ever driven by one section.

Verified in the worktree: `tsc --noEmit` 0 ¬∑ `next lint` clean (changed files) ¬∑ bottom-nav template guard ‚úì. The authenticated `/vendors` surface needs prod env (absent in a fresh worktree) + the "sub-nav-first" beat is timing-dependent locally; the layout-persistence guarantees the order regardless ‚Äî owner to confirm the entry feel on the Vercel preview (per the verification-economy preference).

SPEC IMPACT: minor ‚Äî the Explore/Services takeover's section sub-nav is now mounted in the event layout (ahead of the server panel) rather than inside the takeover; consistent with the `<SubNav>` canonical pattern. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md` (iterations 0021/0022).
## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ chore(nav): drop the orphaned `seating` sidebar slot-key (adversarial-review follow-up to #1595)

A 20-agent adversarial review of #1595 (the desktop sub-nav ‚Üí sidebar change) confirmed the functional logic is correct and **Seating is not orphaned** (still reachable via the Home plan tile, the auto-expanding Guests journey, the mobile bottom nav, and the mobile `<SubNav>`). One clean piece of tech debt it surfaced: `SIDEBAR_SLOT_KEYS['seating']` in `customer-sidebar.tsx` was left behind after the standalone "Seating" Plan item moved under Guests as the nested "Seat" child ‚Äî and `applyRegistry` only walks top-level `group.items`, so the entry is unreachable. Removed it (+ documented why nested children don't get registry overrides yet). Pure dead-code removal; no behavior change. `tsc --noEmit` 0.

Surfaced for owner (NOT changed here): (1) the muted "not yet" Day-of state dims via opacity below WCAG AA ‚Äî a **cross-component** design call (the mobile `<SubNav>` uses the same 0.45 pattern), better solved with a "soon" cue than low contrast; (2) the `customer.guest-journey.*` nav-registry slots are consumed by **neither** the sidebar nor the mobile shelf ‚Äî wiring them is a nav-registry-workstream follow-up that must land on **both** surfaces to avoid drift.

SPEC IMPACT: None (dead-code cleanup; no SKU/pricing/schema/public-surface change). ‚Üí corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ feat(0009/0012): point-of-need Drive connect ‚Äî the 3 deferred surfaces (Papic ¬∑ Recap ¬∑ multi-account)

Owner: "go." Stacked onto the same PR #1597. The three surfaces the first slice deferred, all reusing the shared `DriveSafetyPanel`/`DriveConnectCard`/`DriveReconnectBanner` (relocated `‚Ä¶/photo-delivery/_components/drive-connect-card.tsx` ‚Üí `app/_components/drive-connect-card.tsx` since three surfaces now consume them).

- **Papic storage radio** (`add-ons/papic/page.tsx`): the bare "Connect Google Drive" CTA now leads with the `DriveSafetyPanel` (the consent-screen pre-emption) and says "connect once covers your recap and photographer hand-off too." The connected panel gains the reconnect banner (reads the new `connection_health`) and the account-switch affordance. Storage-target semantics (`setnayan_r2` vs `google_drive_only`) left untouched ‚Äî that drives the capture pipeline.
- **Recap nudge** (`add-ons/papic/recap/page.tsx` + new client `_components/recap-drive-nudge.tsx`): a calm, dismissible champagne-gold "Love this? Save the originals to your Drive" card at the strongest emotional point of need. Server-gated on **recap-has-content AND no live grant AND OAuth-ready**; dismissal is event-scoped in localStorage (a clean opt-out, never a re-show-every-visit nag).
- **Multi-account** (handled at the page level ‚Äî **zero changes to the shared OAuth callback**): each connected panel (Papic + Photo Delivery) already knows both the login email (`user.email`) and the grant email; when they differ it shows a calm "Not your sign-in ‚Äî use a different account" link (couples often connect a shared `ourwedding@gmail.com` on purpose, so this surfaces, never blocks). The "use a different account" path adds `?switch=1` to the start routes ‚Üí new `forceAccountChooser` option on `buildDriveAuthorizeUrl` sets `prompt='select_account consent'` so Google's chooser actually appears (without it Google silently reuses the last session).

Verified: `tsc --noEmit` EXIT=0 ¬∑ `next lint` EXIT=0 (no new-file warnings). Full-env render ‚Üí Vercel preview (local OAuth env unset). No new migration (reuses `20270109000000` from the first slice).

SPEC IMPACT: extends the 0009/0012 point-of-need connect to all three planned couple surfaces; no SKU/pricing/schema change beyond the already-added `connection_health` column. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`.

## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ feat(0009/0012): point-of-need Drive connect ‚Äî safety-led card + "needs reconnect" state

Owner picked "ask at point of need" over bundling Drive into login, then "build it." First slice = the **Photo Delivery** surface + the reconnect flag (the agreed scope; Papic-radio + Recap nudge + multi-account check are follow-ups, reusing the same components).

Ground-truth pass against `origin/main` first (the design research read stale `~/` worktrees): confirmed the Photo Delivery connect routes through the **shared** Drive redirect URI (`getDriveOAuthConfig` ‚Üí `/api/oauth/drive/callback`), so it always writes the single `provider='drive'` grant and stamps the `events.photo_delivery_*` mirror. Both upload paths (`lib/photo-delivery-release.ts` + `lib/drive-copy.ts`) read that one grant ‚Äî so "connect once covers everything" is already true on `main`. The earlier "provider fragmentation" worry was a stale-worktree artifact; the standalone `/api/oauth/photo-delivery/callback` route (writes `provider='drive_photo_delivery'`) is **dead code** never reached by the shared redirect URI (flagged for owner ‚Äî safe to delete in a follow-up).

- **New shared components** (`‚Ä¶/photo-delivery/_components/drive-connect-card.tsx`, presentational server components): `DriveSafetyPanel` (the `drive.file` reassurance that pre-empts Google's consent screen ‚Äî "lists a lot of permissions, that's normal‚Ä¶ we can open only the one wedding folder it makes, never your existing files"), `DriveConnectCard` (benefit headline + body + safety panel + connect CTA + a defer path; Drive framed as an additive "own copy", never a gate; owns the env-gated "setup pending" fallback), and `DriveReconnectBanner` (calm champagne-gold, not alarming red ‚Äî recoverable).
- **Photo Delivery panel** (`photo-delivery-panel.tsx`): replaced the bare "Step 1 of 3 ‚Äî Link your Google Drive" idle card with `DriveConnectCard`; renders `DriveReconnectBanner` on the connected panel when the grant is broken; threaded a `needsReauth` prop. **page.tsx** reads `oauth_grants.connection_health` (provider='drive', RLS-scoped to the couple, same pattern as the Papic page) and passes it down.
- **Reconnect flag (the one backend change)** ‚Äî new migration `20270109000000_oauth_grants_connection_health.sql` adds `oauth_grants.connection_health text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ok' CHECK (‚Ä¶ IN ('ok','needs_reauth'))` (column add inherits existing RLS). Both lazy token refreshers (`getEventDriveAccessToken` in `drive-copy.ts` + the release-path reader in `photo-delivery-release.ts`) previously **swallowed** a Google-side `invalid_grant` (`catch { return null }`) so a revoked token showed "Connected" while uploads silently stalled. They now set `connection_health='needs_reauth'` on failure and clear it to `'ok'` on every successful refresh.

Verified: `tsc --noEmit` EXIT=0 ¬∑ `next lint` EXIT=0 (only pre-existing warnings, none in new files). Full-env render deferred to the Vercel preview (local OAuth env is unset ‚Üí card shows the "setup pending" placeholder). Migration NOT yet applied to prod ‚Äî see OWNER ACTION below.

OWNER ACTION: apply migration `20270109000000` via `supabase db push --db-url "$SUPABASE_DB_URL"` (or it ships with the next batch). Until applied, the `connection_health` reads/writes target a missing column ‚Äî the page read fails soft (banner just never shows); the refresher writes would error in their catch. Low blast radius (Drive OAuth is still admin-gated off in prod), but apply before relying on the reconnect banner.

SPEC IMPACT: iteration 0009 connect-panel copy is replaced with the safety-led point-of-need card, and a new `needs_reauth` reconnect state is added (0009 + 0012 share it). No SKU / pricing change; one additive nullable-default column. Logged in corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`. Surfaced for owner: dead `/api/oauth/photo-delivery/callback` route; the two deferred connect surfaces (Papic radio, Recap nudge) + multi-account confirm; and the two product decisions (per-surface sync-mode default, disconnect‚Üínudge resurfacing) defaulted to auto-sync-from-Papic / review-from-Photo-Delivery and a near-wedding re-surface.
## 2026-06-17 ¬∑ fix(copy): stop claiming face auto-tagging is live (it isn't yet)

Honesty pass alongside the face-auto-tag build (Phases 1+2a shipped the foundation; the recognition engine is still validation-gated). User-facing copy claimed face auto-tagging works *today* ‚Äî it doesn't; tagging is QR-driven. Softened the 7 over-claims to describe the live mechanism (personal/table QR) + the untagged-still-delivered guarantee, dropping the unbuilt face-recognition claims:

- `app/dashboard/[eventId]/add-ons/papic/page.tsx` ‚Äî removed "Auto-face tags fire at ‚â• 0.85 cosine confidence" (a working-feature claim with a number) ‚Üí leads with QR tagging; dropped "face detection" from the Camera Bridge capability line.
- `lib/help.ts` (public help) ‚Äî "auto-tagged via face detection" ‚Üí "tagged to each guest by QR so everyone gets their own".
- `lib/wizard.ts` ‚Äî "auto-tags every photo by face + table QR + scans" ‚Üí "tags photos by personal + table QR so every guest gets theirs".
- `app/page.tsx` + `app/layout.tsx` (homepage + site meta / JSON-LD) ‚Äî "auto-tagged / face-detection auto-tagged galleries" ‚Üí "QR-tagged galleries".

Left as-is (accurate, not over-claims): the RSVP biometric-consent copy ("your photo is set up for face recognition at this wedding" ‚Äî the selfie IS enrolled) and internal code comments.

SPEC IMPACT: iteration 0012 ‚Äî public/dashboard copy no longer promises face auto-tagging before it ships. Logged in corpus DECISION_LOG.md.

## 2026-06-16 ¬∑ feat(editorial): vendor day-of media WRITE PATH ‚Äî submit UI + NSFW screen + migration [Increment 2]

Builds on Increment 1 (the display). The couple's **recommended vendor** can now actually submit day-of media; it auto-shows on the editorial once it clears the NSFW screen.

- **Migration `20270102000000_editorial_vendor_media.sql`** (applied to prod) ‚Äî new event-scoped, vendor-authored table mirroring `event_preparation_items`: `media_type` (photo/clip), `boomerang_r2_key` (clip = baked boomerang, enforced by a CHECK), `still_r2_key` (photo, or clip poster + NSFW proxy), `moderation_state`, `hidden_by_couple`, `event_vendor_id` (the recommended-pick plan row). RLS-at-create: couple SELECT+UPDATE (hide), vendor SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE own (accepted-thread baseline), admin ALL. *(Applied via `db query`/pg per the ledger-drift workaround ‚Äî `db push` was blocked by parallel-session drift; the file's original `20261229000000` collided with a parallel migration so it was renamed to `20270102000000` and the ledger row recorded.)*
- **`lib/editorial-vendor-media.ts`** ‚Äî the eligibility gate `findRecommendedEventVendorId()`: resolves `event_vendors(selection_match_rank=1).service_id ‚Üí vendor_services.vendor_profile_id`, so only the couple's recommended pick qualifies. Plus `MAX_PER_TYPE=3` + `SubmitMediaItem` (kept out of the `'use server'` file).
- **`‚Ä¶/clients/[eventId]/editorial-media/{page.tsx,actions.ts,_components/editorial-media-studio.tsx}`** ‚Äî the vendor submit surface: gated to the recommended pick, add up to 3 photos + 3 clips; **clips trim to ‚â§5s and bake to a boomerang IN THE BROWSER** (same encoder as the couple's Living Hero). The action re-checks the gate + the 3-each cap server-side, inserts via the admin client, and fires the NSFW screen. Existing submissions show a live status (Checking‚Ä¶ / Live / Hidden) with withdraw.
- **`lib/nsfw-screen.ts`** ‚Äî `screenEditorialVendorMedia()` reuses the `classifyImageBytes` + `decideNsfw` primitives; screens the still JPEG (the proxy for both photos and clips). Public editorial **fails closed** for vendor media (only `clean` shows).
- **`editorial/data.ts`** ‚Äî the real-event loader now reads `editorial_vendor_media` (clean + not-hidden + recommended-pick re-checked LIVE so swapping the vendor drops their media) into `vendorMedia`.
- **`‚Ä¶/clients/[eventId]/page.tsx`** ‚Äî an "Add to their editorial" entry card on the Event Brief, shown only to the recommended pick.

Verified: migration live on prod (15 cols, RLS on, 7 policies, CHECK present); `tsc` green; `next lint` clean; the new vendor route compiles (307 ‚Üí login, not 500) and the sample editorial + new DB-loader path render without error.

> **Follow-ups (schema/RLS already support both):** a couple per-item hide control (the `hidden_by_couple` column + couple-UPDATE policy are in place; today they hide the whole strip via the section toggle) and a dedicated admin takedown surface (admin-ALL policy is in place).

SPEC IMPACT: iteration 0046 ‚Äî the editorial gains a vendor-submitted day-of media block (gate = `selection_match_rank=1`, auto-show after NSFW, clips always boomerang). Logged to corpus `DECISION_LOG.md`.

Our rule in editorial should always boomerang."* Owner locked the two pivotal decisions: **eligible = the couple's recommended / first-pick vendor** (`event_vendors.selection_match_rank = 1`), and **auto-show after NSFW moderation** (couple can hide).
